sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.111 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$S$be a finite simple nonabelian group that is not isomorphic to any group${}^2B_2(q)$. A nonidentity automorphism$x$of$S$is called a if every two conjugates of$x$in$x, (S)$generate a subgroup of order not divisible by~$3$. If$S$admits a$$-automorphism, we call$S$a . [r]{(a)} List all$$-groups up to isomorphism. [r]{(b)} Do$$-automorphisms of odd order exist? {{ D.\,O.\,Revin, N.\,Y.\,Yang}}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
